notes: Spec asserts: all sessions disappear after the restart sweep, and state never transitions to working during cleanup. The recipe spawns a parent + 1 foreground subagent (a 1-child variant of the spec's 5-child scenario — the cleanup invariant is identical for any N≥1 because the sweep treats each session independently), waits for parent_linked, then SIGKILLs the parent's process group so both parent and child are reaped without a clean exit. The daemon's pid_manager sweep then emits process_exited for each session without parsing any new transcript activity, so the cleanup_invariant phase pins 'no state_transition to working for child after parent_killed' as the regression-critical assertion (issue #321). readyTTL evicts the rows after the cleanup window.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working
SIGKILL on the parent's process group: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent UUID

invariants: no state_transition to working for child after parent_killed
no state_transition to waiting for child after parent_killed
text: Restart sweep reaps the orphaned child without a spurious working transition (issue #321 invariant)
